LanguageData.Settlers_Wanted.questDescriptionAndNameR: questDescription->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name] hails us. Apparently, the notorious [criminal_nameFull] of [siteFaction_name] is hiding out in our vicinity. \n[asker_nameDef] is asking us to bring [nickname] to one of their settlements for a grand sum of [bounty] silver, double if alive. They are hiding in a safe house and it's probably guarded. \nIf we clear out their camp, [allRewardsDescriptions]
LanguageData.Settlers_Wanted.root.nodes.11.node.nodes: [criminal_nameFull] heard about their compromised position thus have decided to leave the area. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.Settlers_Wanted.root.nodes.12.node.nodes: The brigands has packed up and moved on.
LanguageData.Settlers_Wanted.root.nodes.13.node.custo: You have defeated [criminal_nameFull] and eliminated the camp!\n\nThe bonus reward from [asker_faction_name] has arrived.
LanguageData.VFES_CaravanRaid.questDescriptionRules.r: questDescription->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has sent us a message. Apparently, [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] are resting with their beasts before continuing their journey. \n[asker_nameDef] gave us their location, if we want to, we can go attack them.
LanguageData.VFES_CaravanRaid.root.nodes.10.node.node: The [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] packed up their stuff and moved on. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VFES_CaravanRaid.root.nodes.11.node.node: After being discovered, the [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] left. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has ended.
LanguageData.GR_OpportunitySite_AbandonedLab.questDes: questDescription(askerIsNull==true)->We've intercepted radio transmissions from a seemingly abandoned genetics research lab not far from [map_definite]. The site may have some interesting ancient technology scattered around, so it could be a good idea to investigate it.\n\nWe should be well prepared, as genetic monstrosities may still be roaming the derelict installation.
LanguageData.GR_OpportunitySite_AbandonedLab.root.nod: Intercepted communications indicate the abandoned lab has been scavenged by others. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.GR_OpportunitySite_BiomechanicalLab.ques: questDescription(askerIsNull==true)->The biomechanical lab beacon managed to decode the strange radio signals emanating from inside the strange mechanoid chip, revealing the coordinates of a lab complex not far from [map_definite]. This site seems to have been better hidden than the abandoned labs we have been exploring lately.\n\nThe beacon also revealed the original function of the lab complex: infusing paragon hybrids with reconstructive mechanites to somehow increase their power. Perhaps we should travel to the site with some of these hybrids.
LanguageData.GR_OpportunitySite_BiomechanicalLab.root: Intercepted communications indicate the biomechanical lab has been scavenged by others. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VPE_EltexMeteor.questDescriptionRules.ru: questDescription->A large meteorite has struck ground nearby. It has left behind a lump of eltex. Eltex can be used to create psyfocus-enhancing gear, or sold to the Empire to gain honor. You can send a mining party to retrieve it, but chances are if you wait long enough, someone else will lay claim to it.
LanguageData.VPE_EltexMeteor.root.nodes.6.name: timeoutTicks
Others have mined the Eltex meteor. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
A shuttle has arrived to collect the techfriar from [permitFaction_name].
A laborer sent to you by [permitFaction_name] has died. Your goodwill with [permitFaction_name] has fallen by [goodwillPenaltyPawn].
LanguageData.VFEE_Permit_CallTechfriar.root.nodes.8.t: The techfriar from [permitFaction_name] have arrived. A shuttle will come to collect them in [techfriarDurationDays] days.
LanguageData.VFEE_Permit_CallAbsolver.root.nodes.11.n: An absolver sent to you by [permitFaction_name] has died. A replacement has been issued
LanguageData.VFEE_Permit_CallAbsolver.root.nodes.7.te: The absolver from [permitFaction_name] have arrived. A shuttle will come to collect them in [absolverDurationDays] days.
LanguageData.VFEE_Permit_CallAbsolver.root.nodes.9.no: A shuttle has arrived to collect the absolver from [permitFaction_name].
LanguageData.VFEE_ArtExhibit.questContentRules.rulesS: decidedToLeave->Are leaving immediately, and will not remember this event fondly!
oneOfThe->one of the nobles you invited to the art exhibit

raidArrivedLetterText->A group of deserters have arrived to attempt to disrupt the art exhibit and assasinate the royals\n\n[asker_nameFull] does not care for the threat and demands the exhibit continues! [asker_possessive] wants you too keep them safe and deal with the "peasants".
ArtExhibitSuccessLetterText->The shuttle has departed with [asker_nameFull] and [asker_possessive] guests. Overall the event was a success and everyone made it back safe.
CeremonyFailedLetterText->[asker_nameFull] is disappointed by your failure to make for an enjoyable exhibit. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests
CeremonyTimeoutLetterText->[asker_nameFull] has grown tired of waiting for you to start the exhibit. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests.
LanguageData.VFEE_ArtExhibit.questDescriptionAndNameR:
questDescription->[asker_nameFull], a [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] of the [asker_faction_name] would like to come over with a few other noble people and gaze upon the works of art in your gallery. [asker_pronoun] requests [colonyHost_nameFull] hosts the occasion. This is a great opportunity for noble gossip and to build relations with other members of the imperial nobility.
You are required to have a gallery present in your colony. If at any point the gallery is destroyed, the art exhibition will be called off.
[asker_nameFull] has forwarded you a list of nobility that was invited to the exhibit and will arrive in the shuttle:
[noblesDetailList]

You will need to make sure the guests are safe and comfortable during the exhibition. If anything happens to any of the guests, the art exhibition will be cancelled.
Deserters might use this opportunity to launch an assassination attempt.
joinerEnding->You'll be able to direct [lodgersDef] the same way you direct your own colonists.[specialNeedsExplanation]
LanguageData.VFEE_DelayedArtExhibitOutcome.questConte:
rewardLetterText->[rewardGiver_nameDef] has contacted you. [rewardDelayTicks_duration] ago, you hosted a [outcome] art exhibit for [rewardGiver_possessive] 

Since then, [rewardGiver_pronoun] has gotten back home safe, and was so appreciative [rewardGiver_pronoun] wants to give you a personal gift. [rewardGiver_pronoun] is sending you the following via drop pod:

[listOfRewards]
LanguageData.VFEE_DelayedArtExhibitOutcome.questDescr: questDescription->a delayed reward for art exhibit quest
LanguageData.VFEE_DelayedGrandBallOutcome.questConten:
rewardLetterText->[rewardGiver_nameDef] has contacted you. [rewardDelayTicks_duration] ago, you hosted a [outcome] grand ball for [rewardGiver_possessive] 

Since then, [rewardGiver_pronoun] has gotten back home safe, and was so appreciative [rewardGiver_pronoun] wants to give you a personal gift. [rewardGiver_pronoun] is sending you the following via drop pod:

[listOfRewards]
LanguageData.VFEE_DelayedGrandBallOutcome.questDescri: questDescription->a delayed reward for grand ball quest
LanguageData.VFEE_DeserterHideout.questDescriptionRul:
questDescription-> [asker_nameFull], a [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] of the [asker_faction_name] has informed us about a deserter hideout near [colonyName].
Deserters are a group of ex-imperial soldiers and nobility who have decided to split away from the Great Hierarchy and adapt to the new, post-shattering life. They are considered public enemy number one.
Apparently, [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] have been raiding imperial convoys.
[asker_nameDef] is planning an aerial assault on the hideout, which is guarded by [enemiesCount] [enemiesLabel]. [asker_pronoun] wants you to provide [requiredPawnCount] soldiers to carry out the attack.

[asker_pronoun] will send a shuttle to pick up your fighters, bring them to the attack site, and take them home afterward. They will be fed and medically tended en-route.

Once the attack begins, you must defeat all enemies and turrets within [timeoutTime_duration].

[asker_nameDef] will send [alliesCount] of their personal guards and place them under your command. [asker_pronoun] will not be unhappy if they are killed.
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandBall.questContentRules.rulesSt: CeremonyTimeoutLetterText->[asker_nameFull] has grown tired of waiting for you to start the ball. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests.

raidArrivedLetterText->A group of deserters have arrived to attempt to disrupt the grand ball and assasinate the royals\n\n[asker_nameFull] does not care for the threat and demands the ball continues! [asker_possessive] wants you too keep them safe and deal with the "peasants".
BallSuccessLetterText->The shuttle has departed with [asker_nameFull] and [asker_possessive] guests. Overall the event was a success and everyone made it back safe.
decidedToLeave->Are leaving immediately, and will not remember this event fondly!
oneOfThe->one of the nobles you invited to the grand ball
CeremonyFailedLetterText->[asker_nameFull] is disappointed by your failure to make for an enjoyable Ball. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandBall.questDescriptionAndNameRu:
questDescription->[asker_nameFull], a [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] of the [asker_faction_name] would like you to organise a grand ball. This is a great opportunity for noble gossip and to build relations with other members of the imperial nobility.
You are required to have a ballroom present in your colony. If at any point the ballroom is destroyed or the instruments in it become unusable, the grand ball will be called off
[asker_nameFull] has forwarded you a list of nobility that was invited to the ball and will arrive in the shuttle:
[noblesDetailList]

You will need to make sure the guests are safe and comfortable during the ball. If anything happens to any of the guests, the grand ball will be cancelled. 
Deserters might use this opportunity to launch an assassination attempt.
joinerEnding->You'll be able to direct [lodgersDef] the same way you direct your own colonists.[specialNeedsExplanation]
LanguageData.VFEE_NobleVisit.questContentRules.rulesS: letterIntroBadMood->{SUBJECT_definite}, [oneOfThe], who you were charged to protect and keep happy, has been below the minimum mood of [lodgersMoodThreshold] too long.
lodgerLeftNotAllHealthyLetterText->[lodgersLabel] left the map with injuries and wll not have fond memories of their visit
LetterTextShuttleArrived->The shuttle has arrived to collect [asker_nameFull] and their court
lodgersLeavingLetterText->[asker_nameFull] thanks you for inviting them to stay at [map_definite] for these [questDurationTicks_duration].
decidedToLeave->[othersWillNotAccept], and {PAWNSALIVECOUNT_multiple ? have : has } decided to leave.
othersWillNotAccept->The {PAWNSALIVECOUNT_multiple ? others are : last [lodgersLabel] is} not willing to accept this
oneOfThe->one of the nobles you invited to stay with you
LanguageData.VFEE_NobleVisit.questDescriptionAndNameR: questDescription->[asker_nameFull], a [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] of the [asker_faction_name] would like to perform a customary visit. Invite [asker_possessive], satisfy [asker_possessive] royal needs for [shuttleDelayTicks_duration], and you will gain a large amount of honor. During their visit, you must keep their mood above [lodgersMoodThreshold_percent]. This means providing royal perquisites like a lavish bedroom.\nThe [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] will bring two of [asker_possessive] personal guards and place them under your command. You must house these guards, but the [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] will not complain if they are killed or unhappy.\nYou must keep the visitors at [map_definite] and not send them anywhere else. If you fail this quest or migrate, your relations with [asker_nameFull] will degrade and you will lose honor.

questDescription(nobleCount>=2)->[asker_nameFull], a [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] of the [asker_faction_name] would like to perform a customary visit. Invite [asker_possessive], and [asker_possessive] royal court of [nobleCountLessOne] nobles and satisfy their royal needs for [shuttleDelayTicks_duration], and you will gain a large amount of honor. During their visit, you must keep their mood above [lodgersMoodThreshold_percent]. This means providing royal perquisites like a lavish bedroom.\nThe [asker_nameFull] will bring two of his personal guards and place them under your command. You must house these guards, but the [asker_royalTitleInCurrentFaction] will not complain if they are killed or unhappy.\nYou must keep the visitors at [map_definite] and not send them anywhere else. If you fail this quest or migrate, your relations with [asker_nameFull] will degrade and you will lose honor.
joinerEnding->You'll be able to direct [lodgersDef] the same way you direct your own colonists.[specialNeedsExplanation]
specialNeedsExplanation(asker_royalInCurrentFaction==True,priority=1)-> You'll need to satisfy any special royal needs the [lodgersLabelPlural] may have.
LanguageData.VFEE_Parade.questContentRules.rulesStrin: CeremonyTimeoutLetterText->[asker_nameFull] has grown tired of waiting for you to start the Parade. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests.
raidArrivedLetterText->A group of deserters have arrived to attempt to disrupt the parade and assasinate the [stellarch_nameFull]\n\nThe parade must not be interrupted for any reason!
ParadeSuccessLetterText->The shuttle is ready to depart with all those ascending with [stellarch_nameFull]. It will depart upon your command!
decidedToLeave->Are leaving immediately, and will not remember this event fondly!
oneOfThe->one of the nobles visting for the parade
CeremonyFailedLetterText->[asker_nameFull] is disappointed by your failure to make for an enjoyable Parade. [asker_pronoun] is leaving with [asker_possessive] guests
LanguageData.VFEE_Parade.questDescriptionAndNameRules:
questDescription->[stellarch_nameFull] has reached the peak of what they can achieve from a surface of the rim world. In order to progress further in the grand hierarchy and become the right hand of Emperor [emperor_nameFull], [stellarch_nameFull] needs to partake in a traditional ascension parade, after which they will depart on a shuttle and join the Emperor.
Parade of this magnitude is bound to draw the attention of deserters.
Once you accept this quest, you will have 5 days to prepare for the parade. Consider improving your defenses and working out contingency plans, as it is difficult to say which way the parade will move. At all times you will have to keep the parade safe from harm.
Keep an eye on any rivals or enemies of [stellarch_nameFull], as they might decide it’s a good time to make a move and claim the power for themselves.
The shuttle will arrive bringing the highest ranking nobility to the parade. You will be responsible for providing the security for the event. After the parade starts, it will slowly move through the map for 12 hours, after which everyone, including [stellarch_nameFull], will board the shuttle and depart.
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneStation.questDescriptionRu:
questDescription->Thanks to the intel we have obtained, we now know the location of an aerodrone station belonging to [empire_name]. These stations recharge and reload powerful aerodrones - autonomous, unmanned aerial vehicles that can bombard targets with powerful missile salvos. 
These drones are a threat to everyone who disagrees with the Empire. They need to be destroyed. 
We might find some useful supplies scattered around the station. It's crucial to remember that ammunition stacks and aerodrone batteries are highly volatile, and we might be able to use them to our advantage. 
The aerodrone station is protected by: [forces_description] 
We have also been informed that the location is protected by a number of ground-mounted striker turrets. Despite the limited range, these turrets can easily reveal our approach and provide covering fire for the imperial army. 
The station is protected by surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this mission is to destroy all aerodrones currently stationed here by any means necessary. 
Once we arrive at the station, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterQuestBase.root.nodes.20.nod: The [site_label] has packed up and moved on. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterQuestBase.root.nodes.7.name: siteFaction
LanguageData.VFED_ChasedDeserter.questDescriptionRule:
questDescription->[asker_nameFull], [asker_titleIndef], is calling from nearby. [asker_pronoun] has deserted the army of [asker_factionName] and is being hunted by (*Threat)a loyalty squad(/Threat). [asker_pronoun] wishes to join you at [map_definite]. 
Beware - If you accept [asker_nameDef], you will become an enemy of [asker_factionName]! The pursuing loyalty squad will attack immediately. It is composed of: 

[raid/raidPawnKinds] 
Only accept if you wish to fight against [asker_factionName]. 
Picking the side of the deserters will make the Empire permanently hostile, however it will also give you access to the Deserter network via the comms console. You will be able to gain intel on the Shattered Empire and trade this intel for powerful imperial gear, deserter inventions and new quests. At some point, you will be able to launch assassination missions on fanatic imperial figures in order to destabilize the empire. 
If you have a titled nobility under your control, they will lose all their titles, and with them, requirements for their titles. They will however generate a lump sum of intel for you, and the amount of intel depends on their current rank with Shattered Empire.
LanguageData.VFED_ChasedDeserter.root.nodes.2.name: raidPoints
LanguageData.VFED_ChasedDeserter.root.nodes.7.name: raidDelayTicks
LanguageData.VFED_ChasedDeserter.root.nodes.8.node.no: {BASETEXT}\n\nThis is the loyalty squad that was hunting [../asker_nameFull].
LanguageData.VFED_ControlStationRaid.questDescription:
questDescription->The informant network has relayed us the information of a control station belonging to [empire_name]. These forward-placed outposts survey the area around imperial points of interest and alert them of any imminent threat. 
We might find some useful supplies scattered around the control station. If we have the time, we should take what we can. 
The control station is protected by: [forces_description] 
We have also been informed that the location is protected by a number of ground-mounted striker turrets. Despite the limited range, these turrets can easily reveal our approach and provide covering fire for the imperial army. 
The control station is protected by a large number of surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this assignment is to destroy the surveillance station terminal. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. The terminal is usually well defended and placed inside a fortified building. 
Once we arrive at the control station, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_DeadDrop.questDescriptionRules.rule:
questDescription->We have received a message from our deserter informant that our order is ready for collection not far from [map_definite]. The items we will find there are:

[itemStashContents]

Due to the value of the items and the fact that [empire_name] will inevitably look for them when they realize they’ve gone missing, we should make our way to the dead drop as soon as possible.

We should ensure we have enough carry capacity to bring all the items back.
Intercepted communications indicate the dead drop has been located by [empire_name]. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireBargain.questDescriptionRules:
questDescription->News of the recent assassination of [lastTarget_nameFull] has spread like wildfire across the nobility of [empire_name], fueling the flames of dissent and rebellion. Defectors, once seen as insignificant, have begun seizing victories on various fronts, rattling the gilded cage of the aristocracy. In the shadowy halls of the noble court, whispers stir the air, whispers of desperate bargains offered to the surging rebel forces. 
Intriguingly, one such proposition has found its way to you. Envoys from the imperial court have dispatched an encrypted missive to [map_label]. In carefully coded language, they detail the bounty they're prepared to lavish upon you, should you choose to abandon your deserters' colors: [reward_description] 
If you accept, you will no longer be aligned with the deserters. You will lose access to the Deserter network, and you will never be able to rejoin their ranks again. Your visibility will be lowered to 0 and you will become a member of [empire_name]. You will be able to use all the perks that come from being a noble. Any currently active deserter-related quests will automatically fail. You will also have to face off against a deserter retaliation force, consisting of: [raid/raidPawnKinds] 
It is possible, but not guaranteed, that the Empire will bargain with you again if you continue on your path. Next deals offered might be more bountiful.

questDescription->The untimely demise of [lastTarget_nameFull] has sent shockwaves through the nobility of [empire_name], fanning the embers of dissent into a roaring blaze. What once were dismissed as trivial defections are now victorious battles on numerous fronts, shaking the once secure stronghold of the aristocracy. Amid the hushed murmurings of the noble court, whispers of frantic concessions offered to the rapidly rising rebel forces ripple through the air. 
This proposition is extended to you as well. Emissaries of the empire have dispatched a ciphered communique to [map_label], outlining the considerable benefits you stand to gain, should you decide to forsake the deserters. [reward_description] 
If you accept, you will no longer be aligned with the deserters. You will lose access to the Deserter network, and you will never be able to rejoin their ranks again. Your visibility will be lowered to 0 and you will become a member of [empire_name]. You will be able to use all the perks that come from being a noble. Any currently active deserter-related quests will automatically fail. You will also have to face off against a deserter retaliation force, consisting of: [raid/raidPawnKinds] 
It is possible, but not guaranteed, that the Empire will bargain with you again if you continue on your path. Next deals offered might be more bountiful.

questDescription->Word of [lastTarget_nameFull]'s sudden assassination has cast a chill across the noble circles of [empire_name], kindling a tempest of revolt and insubordination. Those who had once defected, dismissed as inconsequential, are now seizing key victories, causing a seismic shift in the power dynamics of the aristocracy. Secreted away in the labyrinthine depths of the court, rumors of hasty deals offered to the ever-strengthening rebellion echo eerily. 
You are not excluded from this tempting deal. Imperial ambassadors have transmitted a cryptic message to [map_label], shedding light on the incentives you stand to accrue if you abandon the deserters. [reward_description] 
If you accept, you will no longer be aligned with the deserters. You will lose access to the Deserter network, and you will never be able to rejoin their ranks again. Your visibility will be lowered to 0 and you will become a member of [empire_name]. You will be able to use all the perks that come from being a noble. Any currently active deserter-related quests will automatically fail. You will also have to face off against a deserter retaliation force, consisting of: [raid/raidPawnKinds] 
It is possible, but not guaranteed, that the Empire will bargain with you again if you continue on your path. Next deals offered might be more bountiful.

questDescription->The sudden and brutal assassination of [lastTarget_nameFull] has swept through the elite ranks of [empire_name], stoking the flames of insurrection and defiance. Defectors, once scorned as negligible, are achieving unexpected victories, shaking the seemingly impervious bastion of the nobility. In the shadowy corners of the court, murmurs of desperate alliances offered to the surging rebels snake through the air. 
This appealing bargain has also been presented to you. Envoys of the empire have forwarded a concealed missive to your outpost in [map_label], hinting at the boons you could acquire if you dissociate from the deserters. [reward_description] 
If you accept, you will no longer be aligned with the deserters. You will lose access to the Deserter network, and you will never be able to rejoin their ranks again. Your visibility will be lowered to 0 and you will become a member of [empire_name]. You will be able to use all the perks that come from being a noble. Any currently active deserter-related quests will automatically fail. You will also have to face off against a deserter retaliation force, consisting of: [raid/raidPawnKinds] 
It is possible, but not guaranteed, that the Empire will bargain with you again if you continue on your path. Next deals offered might be more bountiful.
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireBargain.root.nodes.4.name: raidPoints
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireBargain.root.nodes.5.name: raidDelayTicks
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireBargain.root.nodes.8.node.par: {BASETEXT}\n\nThis is a retribution force for your betrayal of [../deserters_name] to [../empire_name].
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireRuins.questDescriptionRules.r:
questDescription->The great collapse of [empire_name] was a historic event, one that changed the course of this planet. The once-majestic Imperial flagship, a testament to the Empire's technological prowess, now lies wrecked in a secluded location. 
The debris from this massive ship is scattered across miles of treacherous terrain. Though shattered and dormant, it is believed that buried within its molten metallic embrace lies the last of the imperial technology - techprints, unique devices, and possibly even machinery not yet seen by the world. The potential for advancement, and perhaps dominance, is high for whoever can harness these forgotten wonders. 
Moreover, you can expect this place to be the final resting place of the imperial nobility. The emperor, the most revered figure of the Empire, may have met his end aboard that very ship. If true, his remains, and those of his noble court, may be a treasure trove of their own, revealing secrets or carrying possessions of untold value. 
Time is of the essence. The crash has not gone unnoticed. Other factions, hungry for power and knowledge, are setting their sights on the wreck. If we don’t make haste, we risk losing this opportunity to the winds of time and the ambitions of rivals.
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireRuins.root.nodes.3.name: siteFaction
LanguageData.VFED_EmpireRuins.root.nodes.6.node.nodes: The [site_label] has been stripped by scavengers. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterEndgame.questContentRules.r:
shuttleLetterText->All deserter cells on the planet have been informed about what is going to occur now. Everyone knows that now is the only time to strike. 

A deserter shuttle has landed in your area. It is tasked with delivering a deserter strike force, including whoever you choose to send on this mission, into a Zeus cannon installation. Your soldiers will be assisted by deserter reinforcements. 

The installation is heavily defended. Furthermore, Imperial reinforcements will be dispatched almost immediately upon landing. You can expect the Empire to bring their best and most powerful weapons to try and stop you. 

Your objective is to capture the control room of the Zeus cannon batteries. As long as you are within the range of the main control station, Zeus cannons will continuously fire at the imperial flagship. The target is heavily armored, and bringing it down will require a lot of firepower. 

The installation is defended by an imperial mega high-shield, which should keep you safe from aerodrone bombardments and slicer beams as long as you maintain a constant power supply to it.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterEndgame.questDescriptionRul:
questDescription->[noble_nameFull] is the Emperor of the [empire_name]. An egocentric ruler who values tradition over the lives of [noble_possessive] people, [noble_pronoun] has to be stopped at all costs. [noble_pronoun] refuses to adapt to the new reality post-Sophiamunda Shattering, and instead insists on maintaining old ways, leading to the wasteful loss of countless lives. 

Despite the warnings we have given [noble_objective] and the obvious signs of impending doom, [noble_pronoun] remains obstinate in [noble_possessive] ways, driving the Empire towards certain destruction. The only hope for the deserters is to take out the Imperial flagship in orbit, which gives the Emperor an overwhelming military advantage. 

In order to do that, we have to take control of three Zeus cannons located in various places on the planet. This mission needs to be executed on all fronts simultaneously, as we will only have a limited window of opportunity once the first Zeus cannon is taken. Activating all three of them at the same time will ensure the total obliteration of the Imperial flagship, after which the Empire will have no choice but to dissolve.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterEndgame.root.nodes.10.name: siteFaction
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterEndgame.root.nodes.21.label: shuttle arrived
The shuttle sent to collect colonists has been destroyed.
The zeus cannon control station has been destroyed.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterEndgame.root.nodes.5.name: pointsOriginal
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialConvoy.questDescriptionRule:
questDescription->We have been informed of a maxpack convoy belonging to [empire_name] in our vicinity. The convoy had to make an emergency stop, which is a perfect opportunity for us to raid it. Whilst maxpacks will be impervious to any break-in attempts, there is bound to be a lot of other valuables scattered around the trucks. 
We need to eliminate everyone in the convoy before they move on. 
The convoy is protected by: [forces_description] 
During unexpected stops like this, Empire is known to place strike buoys around the temporary camps. These strike buoys will alert the Empire of our location, and will trigger a nearby aerodrone to fire missiles at the buoy location. We should make it our priority to avoid these traps. 
The objective of this assignment is to eliminate all enemy personnel in the area. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. 
Once we arrive at the convoy location, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_PlotMission.questContentRules.rules: raid/approach_desc->[noble_nameFull] is residing in their mansion near [map_label]. The structure is heavily fortified and well defended. Raiding this mansion will draw the least attention to us, but will also mean the hardest fight.

shootDown/approach_desc->[noble_nameFull] is travelling in a shuttle near [map_label]. Other deserters agreed to bring the shuttle down near us, but we will have to venture to the crashed shuttle site and finish them off.

falseInvitation/approach_desc->Using our connections, we can invite [noble_nameFull] to [map_label]. They will think they are performing a customary noble visit, when in reality they will walk into their doom. This will definitely draw attention to us.
LanguageData.VFED_PlotMission.questDescriptionRules.r: questDescription(nobleTitle==Knight)->[noble_nameFull] is a knight of [empire_name], infamous for the brutal tactics and ruthless methods of extracting information from prisoners. [noble_pronoun] gained [noble_possessive] name due to the atrocious acts [noble_pronoun] commits in the name of the Emperor, leaving a trail of destruction in their wake. \nEliminating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant victory for the deserter cause, as [noble_pronoun] is responsible for the capture and interrogation of many of our sympathizers. Completing this mission would disrupt the Empire's intelligence-gathering operations and hinder their ability to track and locate the deserter cells.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Praetor)->[noble_nameFull] is a praetor of [empire_name], known for [noble_possessive] small-minded and vindictive nature, often punishing subordinates for minor infractions and taking pleasure in exerting [noble_possessive] power over others. [noble_pronoun] gained [noble_possessive] moniker due to [noble_possessive] tendency to focus on petty grievances rather than more significant issues. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant boon for the deserters, as [noble_pronoun] is responsible for overseeing the occupation of several key settlements and has a reputation for being particularly harsh on dissenters. Removing [noble_objective] from power would weaken Imperial control in the region and potentially inspire other subjects to rise up against their oppressors.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_Despot)->[noble_nameFull] is a despot of [empire_name] who holds a high position in the Imperial court and is known for [noble_possessive] madness and depravity. [noble_pronoun] earned this moniker due to [noble_possessive] reputation for sadistic and cruel behavior, including forcing numerous subjects to serve in [noble_possessive] personal pleasure house, where disobedience is punished with death. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a crucial victory for the deserter cause, as [noble_possessive] removal from power would not only disrupt the political balance but also remove a key figure responsible for the inhumane treatment of the people. [noble_possessive] death would also likely weaken the morale of the Imperial forces and potentially inspire rebellion among the oppressed masses.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Stellarch)->[noble_nameFull] is a powerful and feared stellarch of [empire_name], known for [noble_possessive] ruthless tactics and eugenic beliefs. [noble_pronoun] earned [noble_possessive] moniker due to [noble_possessive] reputation for forcing euthanasia on any noble children with any sort of deformities or disabilities, a practice that is cruel, inhumane and wrong. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a major blow to the Imperial regime, as it would remove a key figure responsible for the perpetuation of eugenic practices and the persecution of the disabled. It would also strike a blow against the Imperial propaganda machine, which claims that the regime is promoting "genetic purity" for the betterment of the empire. Completing this mission will bring the final mission into our grasp.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_HighStellarch)->[noble_nameFull] is a high stellarch of [empire_name] and is notorious for ruthless tactics and cruel treatment of the citizens under [noble_possessive] rule, often ordering mass executions, divine inferno bombardments and harsh punishments for even the slightest infractions. [noble_pronoun] is feared and despised by many who live under [noble_possessive] jurisdiction. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would severely cripple the Empire's military and political power, as [noble_pronoun] is one of the Emperor's most trusted advisors and commanders. Additionally, it would inspire rebellion and resistance among the people who have long suffered under his brutal regime. Completing this mission will set the stage for our final mission, exposing three locations of Zeus cannons that we will need to bring the imperial flagship down.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Baron)->[noble_nameFull] is a baron of [empire_name], known for [noble_possessive] prejudiced views and discriminatory policies towards non-baseliners. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to intolerant and narrow-minded worldview, which has caused great suffering and injustice for many under [noble_possessive] rule. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a crucial victory for the deserters, as [noble_pronoun] is a key figurehead of the Empire's anti-xeno policies and a driving force behind their efforts to suppress and subjugate non-baseliners. [noble_possessive] death would send a message to other Imperial leaders that the deserters will not stand for such bigotry and discrimination.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Count)->[noble_nameFull] is a count of [empire_name], known for [noble_possessive] corrupt and scheming ways, using wealth and influence to manipulate politics and advance [noble_possessive] own interests. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to dishonest and unethical behavior, often engaging in underhanded dealings and double-crossing those who oppose [noble_objective]. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant blow to the Empire, as [noble_pronoun] is a key figure in the Imperial court and wields a great deal of power and influence. [noble_possessive] death would disrupt the political balance and potentially spark infighting among rival factions, weakening the Empire's cohesion and ability to respond to deserter threats.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_Archcount)->[noble_nameFull] is an archcount of [empire_name] notorious for [noble_possessive] sadistic tendencies and cruel treatment of those under [noble_possessive] command. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to abusive behavior, which has left a trail of broken lives and shattered spirits in [noble_possessive] wake. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant victory for the deserters, as [noble_pronoun] is responsible for overseeing the construction of several key Imperial fortifications and the management of labor camps where prisoners are subjected to horrific conditions. [noble_possessive] death would disrupt the Empire's construction projects and potentially inspire uprisings among the oppressed laborers.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_Marquess)->[noble_nameFull] is a marquess of [empire_name] known for [noble_possessive] aggressive and militaristic approach to governance, advocating for the expansion of the Empire's military might and the suppression of dissenting voices. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to fervent dedication to military might and discipline, and belief that war is the solution to all problems. \nEliminating [noble_nameFull] would be a crucial victory for the deserters, as [noble_pronoun] is a key proponent of the Empire's militarization efforts and a driving force behind their expansionist policies. [noble_possessive] death would disrupt the Empire's plans for conquest and potentially dissuade other leaders from pursuing similar aggressive strategies.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Duke)->[noble_nameFull] is a duke of [empire_name] known for hedonistic lifestyle and depraved behavior, using wealth and influence to indulge in all manner of vices and debauchery. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to immoral and corrupt behavior, often using power to exploit and abuse those beneath him. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant blow to the Empire, as [noble_pronoun] is a key figure in the Imperial court and wields a great deal of power and influence. [noble_possessive] death would disrupt the political balance and potentially create openings for deserter spies and sympathizers to gain valuable intelligence.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_Archduke)->[noble_nameFull] is an archduke of [empire_name] known for [noble_possessive] haughty and dismissive attitude towards those [noble_pronoun] deems beneath [noble_objective], often belittling and insulting [noble_possessive] subordinates and opponents alike. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to overwhelming sense of entitlement and superiority, which has earned [noble_objective] a reputation as a difficult and unpleasant figure to work with. \n[noble_nameFull] is widely disliked among [noble_possessive] peers, who would likely welcome a change of command and defect to our cause, providing valuable resources and information. [noble_possessive] death would also serve as a warning to other Imperial leaders that the deserters will not tolerate such arrogance and disrespect towards others, potentially inspiring imperial soldiers to join the deserter cause and overthrow the oppressive Empire.

questDescription(nobleTitle==Consul)->[noble_nameFull] is a consul of [empire_name] who holds a high position in the Imperial court and is known for [noble_possessive] unethical and immoral behavior. [noble_pronoun] gained this moniker due to reputation for bribery, embezzlement, and other corrupt practices, which [noble_pronoun] uses to maintain [noble_possessive] position and influence with the Emperor. \n[noble_nameFull] is widely disliked among the people, who suffer from [noble_possessive] corrupt practices and the resulting economic inequality and injustice. [noble_possessive] death would likely inspire rebellion among oppressed populations and potentially sway other members of the Imperial court to defect to the deserter cause, providing valuable resources and support in the fight against the Empire.

questDescription(nobleTitle==VFEE_Magister)->[noble_nameFull] is a magister of [empire_name] who holds a high position in the Imperial court and is known for [noble_possessive] ruthless and oppressive tactics. [noble_pronoun] gained [noble_possessive] moniker due to the reputation for decimating [noble_possessive] own subjects in order to maintain obedience and instill fear in the rest of the population. \nAssassinating [noble_nameFull] would be a significant blow to the Empire and a major victory for the deserters, bringing us one step closer to the final mission. [noble_possessive] death would not only disrupt the political balance but also remove a key figure responsible for the brutal oppression and subjugation of the people.
LanguageData.VFED_PlotMission.root.nodes.6.name: pointsOriginal
LanguageData.VFED_PlotMission.root.nodes.8.title: $nobleTitle
LanguageData.VFED_ShuttleStagingPost.questDescription:
questDescription->Our intel allowed us to pinpoint the location of a shuttle staging post belonging to [empire_name]. These staging posts allow shuttles to refuel between destinations. Destroying it will greatly hinder the Empire’s ability to freely travel the planet and resupply their forward bases. 
We may be able to trigger a chain detonation of the fuel containers to create havoc and make our job easier. 
We might find some useful supplies scattered around the staging post. If we have the time, we should take what we can. 
The shuttle staging post is protected by: [forces_description] 
We have also been informed that the location is protected by a number of ground-mounted striker turrets. Despite the limited range, these turrets can easily reveal our approach and provide covering fire for the imperial army. 
The staging post is protected by surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this assignment is to destroy all fuel containers. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. 
Once we arrive at the staging post, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_SupplyDepotRaid.questDescriptionRul:
questDescription->The intel we have gathered allowed us to gain insight into the location of a supply depot belonging to [empire_name]. Supply depots like that are often scattered around imperial settlements and serve as secure stockpiles for the most valuable resources. Keeping these supply depots hidden away discourages any raiders from attacking these settlements. 
The compound is guaranteed to contain numerous supply crates. Stealing these items is bound to slow down the Imperial war effort. 
It is also possible we will find additional Biosecured crates, which can only be opened by noble-born people. 
The supply depot is protected by: [forces_description] 
The compound is protected by surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this assignment is to open all supply crates in the area. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. 
Once we arrive at the compound, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_TechprinterDemolition.questDescript:
questDescription->We have received the news of a techprinter installation belonging to [empire_name] near our location. Techprinters are a huge environmental hazard, and are used by techfriars to replicate their knowledge on techprints. 
Techite discs used in this process are extremely toxic and flammable. We might be able to ignite a stack of these discs and see the inferno unfold. 
The techprinter installation is protected by: [forces_description]. 
Two palintone turrets are located at the installation. They are powerful, anti-infantry weapons firing large-caliber shells at incredible speeds, dealing heavy damage over significant ranges. Their ammo needs to be stored right next to them, and a precise shot might set off a chain explosion. 
We have also been informed that the location is protected by a number of ground-mounted striker turrets. Despite the limited range, these turrets can easily reveal our approach and provide covering fire for the imperial army. 
Two techfriars are most certainly at the installation. Killing them is not necessary. 
The techprinter installation is protected by a number of surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this assignment is to destroy the techprinters by any means necessary. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. 
Once we arrive at the installation, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialUnderfarmRaid.questDescript:
questDescription->Our intel suggests that [empire_name] has an underfarm complex near our location. Underfarms provide imperial settlements with a steady supply of food. Destroying this underfarm complex is bound to cause unrest in the lower ranks, as food becomes scarce. 
The location features huge swathes of empty terrain with no cover in sight. We should think how we want to approach this. 
The underfarm complex is protected by: [forces_description]. 
A kontarion turret is located at the installation. It’s a powerful, long-range weapon used by the Empire to take out enemy targets from afar. Kontarion turret fires ultra-dense uranium slugs at incredible speeds, penetrating even the heaviest armor. Its ammo needs to be stored right next to it, and a precise shot might set off a chain explosion. 
The underfarm complex is protected by a number of surveillance pillars. They should not be approached, and instead should be disabled from afar - either by destroying them, or cutting off their power. Once activated, they will greatly increase our visibility. 
The objective of this assignment is to destroy the water mains that supply the underfarms. Once that is done, the reward will arrive and we will have to make our escape. 
Once we arrive at the complex, we can expect an imperial response if our visibility is high enough. We will only have a limited amount of time to complete the mission.
LanguageData.VFET_OpportunitySite_WildMen.questDescri: questDescription-> [asker_nameFull] of [asker_faction_name], found out about an existence of a small tribe of wild men near [map_definite]. Wild men most likely mean us no harm, and are gathering together hoping to that the numbers will help them survive. \nWe may decide to venture to the gathering in order to crush the wild men. Alternatively, we may leave them be. In time, these wild men may find their way to us - depending on their numbers, they may choose to join us, or attack us instead. [asker_nameFull] has a feeling that the small tribe consists of [ListofPawns].
LanguageData.AM_Quest_Mall_L.questDescriptionRules.ru: questDescription -> We have discovered an ancient giant shopping mall that is remarkably well-preserved and abundant in resources. Undoubtedly, other factions have also discovered this location, making a conflict inevitable.
The ancient architectural complex has been searched and wiped out, and it will disappear into the tide of history.
LanguageData.AM_Quest_Mall_S.questDescriptionRules.ru: questDescription -> We have discovered an ancient giant shopping mall that is remarkably well-preserved and abundant in resources. Undoubtedly, other factions have also discovered this location, making a conflict inevitable.
The ancient architectural complex has been searched and wiped out, and it will disappear into the tide of history.
LanguageData.AM_Quest_Reserve.questDescriptionRules.r: questDescription -> We have discovered the location of an ancient military Reservation Station, which is heavily fortified and rumored to be manned by well-equipped ancient elite soldiers. Infiltrating this location carries extreme risk, but also offers equally significant rewards.
LanguageData.AM_Quest_Reserve.root.nodes.1.node.nodes: The ancient architectural complex has been searched and wiped out, and it will disappear into the tide of history.
LanguageData.AM_Quest_Street.questDescriptionRules.ru: questDescription -> We have discovered the site of an ancient block ruins, where the streets were once lined with shops. The map also indicates the presence of an entrance to an underground commercial street, promising the discovery of numerous high-value items.
The ancient architectural complex has been searched and wiped out, and it will disappear into the tide of history.
LanguageData.ACM_AncientRandomSite.questDescriptionRu: questDescription->We've discovered the location of an abandoned construction site, rumored to hold an entrance to the ancient city's underground system. A group of guards, clad in equipment stolen from an old bunker, are stationed there. They appear oblivious to the fact that they've been discovered.
LanguageData.VQE_PrototypeARC.questContentRules.rules: terminalStudiedMessage->Terminal studied. You've collected the information and can now leave.
LanguageData.VQE_PrototypeARC.questDescriptionRules.r: questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has informed us of an ancient construction site where [inventor_nameFull] once worked on a massive prototype generator. The site appears abandoned and isolated, surrounded by the remnants of a long-forgotten project.\n\n[terminalDetails] Visiting this site may provide [potentialRewards]. [journeyDetails] [finalDetails]

questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->We have discovered an ancient construction site where [inventor_nameFull] was working on a large prototype generator. The site lies in a remote location, surrounded by the ruins of an unfinished project.\n\n[terminalDetails] Exploring the site may yield [potentialRewards]. [journeyDetails] [finalDetails]
potentialRewards->critical research breakthroughs and a bounty of salvageable materials
potentialRewards->rare loot and potentially groundbreaking energy research
journeyDetails->The location is far away and will require careful preparation for the trip.
journeyDetails->The long distance to the site makes the expedition challenging but potentially rewarding.
journeyDetails->Traveling to the site will demand significant time and resources.
journeyDetails->The journey to this remote site may be dangerous but worth the effort.
journeyDetails->Its remote location suggests few immediate threats, but supplies will be key.
finalDetails->While no dangers are expected, the journey itself poses risks to unprepared colonists.
finalDetails->The site seems derelict, but caution is advised—its isolation may hide unexpected challenges.
finalDetails->The area appears uninhabited, but its age may make navigation and survival difficult.
terminalDetails->There is a still-operational terminal containing research notes and schematics of [inventor_nameFull]'s work.
finalDetails->Prepare for the worst while hoping for the best—opportunity often comes with risk.
finalDetails->The isolation may provide safety, but also leaves little room for error if trouble arises.
terminalDetails->An active terminal holds critical data logs and experimental insights into generator construction.
terminalDetails->The site contains a terminal rumored to have design blueprints and energy research breakthroughs.
terminalDetails->Ancient systems still hum faintly, offering a glimpse into [inventor_nameFull]'s ambitious prototype.
terminalDetails->A damaged terminal may still hold data on the ARC prototype, but its condition is uncertain.
potentialRewards->rare components, advanced research data, and valuable resources
potentialRewards->essential materials, generator schematics, and advanced technology insights
potentialRewards->valuable knowledge, unique building materials, and rare artifacts
LanguageData.VQE_AbandonedARC.questContentRules.rules: allObjectsStudiedMessage->All objects are studied. You've collected the information and can now leave.
halfOfObjectsDestroyedMessage->More than half of the research data has been lost.
LanguageData.VQE_AbandonedARC.questDescriptionRules.r:
questDescription->According to the data extracted on the old prototype site, we managed to find the location of an [adj] industrial complex where construction of the [arcName] continued. This sprawling facility was once bustling with engineers and scientists pushing the boundaries of technology, but now it stands in silence, overtaken by the passage of time.

The site contains [siteDetails], but it is now [siteThreat].

[studyWarning] [questFailWarning]

Successfully completing this expedition [questOutcome].

questDescription->Data from the prototype site revealed the coordinates of an [adj] industrial yard, where the [arcName] was further developed by [inventor_nameFull] and their team. The facility stretches across a vast expanse, its rusting frames and crumbling walls hinting at its former glory.

Within the complex are [siteDetails], but [siteThreat] must be dealt with before any meaningful research can be done.

[studyWarning] [questFailWarning]

Resolving this task [questOutcome].

questDescription->Analyzing the prototype’s data, we’ve pinpointed the location of an [adj] construction yard that served as the next step in the [arcName] project. This site, once a beacon of industrial ambition, is now a graveyard of innovation, its machinery left to decay under the open sky.

Inside, you’ll find [siteDetails], but it is [siteThreat].

[studyWarning] [questFailWarning]

Your efforts here [questOutcome].

questDescription->The data retrieved from the old prototype has led us to an [adj] industrial complex that was pivotal in advancing the [arcName] project. Once a thriving hub of technological development, the facility now lies in ruin, its halls echoing only with the wind.

Among the remnants are [siteDetails], though the complex has become [siteThreat].

[studyWarning] [questFailWarning]

Completing this mission [questOutcome].

siteThreat->occupied by [Number] hostile [AnimalPlural]

studyWarning->To gain insights, you'll need to investigate the research notes and generator parts thoroughly.
studyWarning->Thorough investigation is key to retrieving useful data from the site.
studyWarning->The data here could be critical, so every effort must be made to study the area thoroughly.
questFailWarning->If more than half of the studiable machines and notes are destroyed, the mission will fail.
questFailWarning->Too much damage to the complex will result in failure.
questFailWarning->Destroying too many research materials will cause the mission to fail.

LanguageData.VQE_AncientARC.questContentRules.rulesSt: terminalStudiedMessage->Terminal studied. You've collected the information and can now leave.
LanguageData.VQE_AncientARC.questDescriptionRules.rul:
questDescription->You have [discoveryDesc] of a [adj] village where the potentially final prototype of [inventor_nameFull]'s [arcName] has been built. The villagers [villagerBehavior], and the site [villageCondition].

[arcDetails] However, [villagerWarnings]. If [trigger], the villagers will [villagerReaction].

[finalWarning]
villageCondition->shows signs of past technological greatness
villageCondition->has long since fallen from its peak
villageCondition->bears the scars of time and neglect
arcDetails->The [arcName] is said to hold valuable secrets for your colony.
arcDetails->Studying the [arcName] could unlock vital knowledge.
arcDetails->This ancient [arcName] may reveal the means to build one yourself.
villagerWarnings->be careful not to provoke the villagers
villagerWarnings->the villagers will not take kindly to trespassers

villagerWarnings->their patience may be thin with outsiders

LanguageData.VQE_InventorShelter.questDescriptionRule: questDescription->[dataDiscovery] revealed the location of a [adj] underground [structureType] where [inventor_nameFull] rests in cryptosleep. The journey will be grueling, as the site is afflicted by a[coldSnapDesc], bringing the region into unnaturally low temperatures. \n\nInside the bunker, a few [turretDefense] still guard the interior, powered by an ancient [arcName]. The ARC cannot be studied but can be deconstructed for [arcComponents]. Your primary task, however, is to reach the casket and release the inventor. Prepare carefully for the extreme cold or risk severe [hypothermiaDanger].

questDescription->Recovered data points us to a [adj] bunker buried deep beneath a region suffering from a[coldSnapDesc]. It is here that [inventor_nameFull], the legendary mind behind the ARC project, lies sealed in cryptosleep. \n\nThe area’s temperatures are falling to dangerous lows, creating an immediate risk of [hypothermiaDanger] during your approach. Inside, [turretDefense] remain active, powered by an overgrown [arcName]. The ARC can be deconstructed for [arcComponents], but the real prize is freeing the inventor and bringing them back to your colony.

questDescription->We’ve identified the location of a [adj] [structureType] where [inventor_nameFull] has been preserved in a cryptosleep casket. The area surrounding the site is cursed with a[coldSnapDesc], plummeting temperatures to deadly extremes. \n\nThe bunker itself houses a few [turretDefense], powered by an overgrown [arcName]. While the ARC cannot be studied, it can be salvaged for [arcComponents]. Be ready for freezing conditions and hypothermia as you attempt to retrieve the legendary inventor.
turretDefense->inactive turrets that occasionally sputter to life
turretDefense->a handful of aging security turrets
turretDefense->old but still lethal automated turrets
turretDefense->a few crumbling yet operational turrets
LanguageData.VQE_ArcSite.questDescriptionRules.rulesS: questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has sent us a message. A group of [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] has occupied the location of an abandoned ARC generator site. The camp is under the control of [siteFaction_name].\n\n[asker_nameDef] is requesting that we clear out the area by eliminating all enemies and defenses. Once the site is secured, we will have the opportunity to study the ARC generator and recover valuable ARC components. [asker_label] also says that [sitePart0_description].

questDescription(askerIsNull==true)->We have received information that an old ARC generator site has been taken over by [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] from [siteFaction_name]. The bandits have fortified their position and are using the location as a hideout.\n\nSecuring the site will allow us to examine the generator for any remaining research data and salvageable ARC components. Be prepared for resistance, as the site is likely heavily guarded. Additionally, [sitePart0_description].
sitePart0_description->the site is partially overgrown, with many of its original structures crumbling from age
sitePart0_description->the bandits have fortified the site with makeshift defenses and traps
sitePart0_description->the ARC generator is visibly damaged but still holds valuable salvage
sitePart0_description->the site appears to be a mix of old ruins and hastily built bandit encampments
sitePart0_description->hostile patrols have been seen guarding the perimeter
sitePart0_description->the bandits are using the generator to power their crude operations
LanguageData.VQE_ArcSite.root.nodes.0.nodes.0.name: askerIsNull
LanguageData.VQE_ArcSite.root.nodes.2.node.nodes.0.te: The bandit camp has packed up and moved on. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
LanguageData.VQE_ArcSite.root.nodes.3.node.nodes.0.te: After being discovered, the bandit camp has dispersed. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has ended.
LanguageData.VQE_ArcSite.root.nodes.4.node.nodes.1.no: You have defeated the bandit camp!\n\nThe payment from [asker_faction_name] has arrived.
LanguageData.VQE_NuclearArcSite.questDescriptionRules: questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has sent us a message. A group of [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] has taken control of an abandoned nuclear ARC husk site. The camp is currently under the control of [siteFaction_name].\n\n[asker_nameDef] is requesting that we clear out the area by eliminating all enemies and defenses. Once the site is secured, we will have the opportunity to study the nuclear ARC husk and recover valuable ARC components. [asker_label] also mentions that [sitePart0_description]. [sitePart1_description]

questDescription(askerIsNull==true)->We have received intelligence that an abandoned nuclear ARC husk site has been overtaken by [siteFaction_pawnsPlural] from [siteFaction_name]. The bandits have fortified their position and are using the location as a hideout.\n\nSecuring the site will allow us to examine the nuclear ARC husk for any remaining research data and salvageable ARC components. Be prepared for resistance, as the bandits have likely set up significant defenses. Additionally, [sitePart0_description]. [sitePart1_description]
sitePart1_description->Toxic fallout from the ARC husk’s old reactor has poisoned the area, presenting a significant environmental danger
sitePart1_description->The site is covered in toxic fallout, making survival without protective gear extremely difficult
sitePart1_description->Hazardous fallout clouds the area, a grim reminder of the ARC’s unstable nuclear past
sitePart0_description->the nuclear ARC husk is surrounded by overgrowth, with much of its structure showing signs of decay
sitePart0_description->the bandits have heavily fortified the area with makeshift barriers and traps
sitePart0_description->the husk appears partially intact, holding the promise of valuable salvage
sitePart0_description->the site is a mix of ancient industrial ruins and ramshackle bandit encampments
sitePart0_description->hostile patrols have been spotted guarding the perimeter
sitePart0_description->the bandits are using the ARC husk to power their crude machinery and weapons
sitePart1_description->The entire area is blanketed in toxic fallout, making prolonged exposure dangerous without proper equipment
sitePart1_description->A thick layer of toxic fallout lingers over the site, creating an additional hazard for anyone attempting to approach

Securing the site will allow us to study the geothermal ARC and recover valuable components for future use. Additionally, [sitePart0_description].

LanguageData.VFEM2_OpportunitySite_SiegeCamp.questDes:
questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has informed us about a siege camp belonging to [siteFaction_name], set up near your location. They seem to be gathering numbers and will imminently attack your colony at [map_definite].
If you choose to attack preemptively, you will only need to face:
[ListOfEnemies]

If you wait, they are bound to increase their numbers and will eventually attack you with a much larger force:
[ListOfPotentialEnemies]

questDescription(askerIsNull==true)->You have found out that there is a siege camp belonging to [siteFaction_name], set up near your location. They seem to be gathering numbers and will imminently attack your colony at [map_definite].
If you choose to attack preemptively, you will only need to face:
[ListOfEnemies]

If you wait, they are bound to increase their numbers and will eventually attack you with a much larger force:
[ListOfPotentialEnemies]
LanguageData.VFEM2_OpportunitySite_SiegeCamp.root.nod: You have successfully defeated the siege camp and the enemy forces!
The siege camp was not defeated in time. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired, and the enemy forces are now raiding your colony.
LanguageData.VFEM2_OpportunitySite_Skirmish.questDesc:
questDescription->[asker_nameFull], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has requested our help in a skirmish against [siteFaction_name]. 
A small force from [askerFaction_name] is engaging a larger group of [siteFaction_pawnsPlural]. The friendlies will attack immediately upon arrival, but their chances of victory without our help are slim. 
This is an opportunity to strengthen our bond with [askerFaction_name]. However, the enemy force is significant, and this battle will not be without risk. 

Allied forces include:
[FriendlyUnitList] 

Enemies from [siteFaction_name]:
[EnemyUnitList]
LanguageData.VFEM2_OpportunitySite_Skirmish.root.node: The allied faction was defeated in the skirmish. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has expired.
After leaving the site, the battle resolved itself without your help. The quest [resolvedQuestName] has ended.
You have defeated the enemy forces!\n\nThe payment from [asker_faction_name] has arrived.

These colonists participated in the victory for the quest [resolvedQuestName]. [asker_definite] wants to know who should receive the [royalFavorReward_amount] [asker_faction_royalFavorLabel] for this service.
LanguageData.VQE_Cryptoforge_Chapter1.questDescriptio:
questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has contacted us with news of a long-lost starship. Generations ago, a legendary vessel belonging to a nomadic warrior fleet crashed on this planet, its crew renowned for wielding cryptoparticles in battle. The cryptoforge ship shattered upon atmospheric entry, breaking into three massive fragments scattered across the surface. To locate them, we must reactivate an ancient scanning relay station. 

[asker_nameDef] says that initiating the scans will allow us to pinpoint the locations of the ship’s stern and bow, which may hold further clues to the whereabouts of the final, most crucial fragment. However, the relay station remains heavily secured - automated turrets still stand watch, and the surrounding grounds are riddled with a dense minefield. Worse yet, the power surge from the scan is certain to draw the attention of nearby mechanoids.

Complete the scans on both relays to discover the location of the ship fragments.

questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->We've uncovered information about a long-lost starship. Generations ago, a legendary vessel belonging to a nomadic warrior fleet crashed on this planet, its crew renowned for wielding cryptoparticles in battle. The cryptoforge ship shattered upon atmospheric entry, breaking into three massive fragments scattered across the surface. To locate them, we must reactivate an ancient scanning relay station. 

Initiating the scans will allow us to pinpoint the locations of the ship’s stern and bow, which may hold further clues to the whereabouts of the final, most crucial fragment. However, the relay station remains heavily secured - automated turrets still stand watch, and the surrounding grounds are riddled with a dense minefield. Worse yet, the power surge from the scan is certain to draw the attention of nearby mechanoids.

Complete the scans on both relays to discover the location of the ship fragments.
LanguageData.VQE_Cryptoforge_Chapter2.questDescriptio:
questDescription->Data retrieved from the ancient relay station confirms the crash site of the cryptoforge ship’s stern, located not far from the scanning facility. While this section does not contain the legendary cryptoforge itself, it holds a crucial black box. To pinpoint the final cryptoforge fragment, we must retrieve its data and pair it with the bow’s black box records. 

The wreckage scan reveals a high concentration of dormant mechanoids aboard, making conflict likely. The ship’s hull is built from near-indestructible material, limiting possible entry points. Ancient airlocks might still be operational, though forcing them open will require effort. Some external turrets remain active, further complicating the approach. Inside, automated floor freezers keep the corridors at dangerously low temperatures, serving as a defensive measure against intruders. 

Despite these challenges, the wreckage contains valuable resources. Extracting as much salvage as possible will be essential for reactivating the cryptoforge and crafting the legendary cryptogear.

According to the scan, the ship is protected by at least:
[ListOfEnemies]
LanguageData.VQE_Cryptoforge_Chapter3.questDescriptio:
questDescription->Data recovered from the ancient relay station has revealed the crash site of the cryptoforge ship’s bow, located not far from the scanning relay’s position. While this section does not house the cryptoforge itself, it contains a vital black box. To locate the final ship fragment, we must retrieve its data and pair it with the information from the stern’s black box. 

Scans indicate a significant presence of insectoid lifeforms infesting the wreckage, making combat inevitable if we wish to reach the bridge where the black box is stored. The ship’s hull is composed of nearly indestructible material, limiting our means of entry. Some ancient airlocks may still function, but forcing them open will be necessary. A weak energy signature has also been detected within - likely from a dormant reactor. If reactivated, it may power the ship’s old floor heaters, weakening the cold-adapted insectoids inside.

Although the cryptoforge itself is not housed within this wreckage, valuable resources remain, fused and reshaped by the ship’s violent descent. Extracting these materials will aid in restoring the cryptoforge and crafting the legendary cryptogear. Studying the black box should be our top priority.
LanguageData.VQE_Cryptoforge_Chapter4.questDescriptio:
questDescription->After analyzing the data from both black boxes, we have finally pinpointed the location of the last ship fragment - home to the legendary cryptoforge. This ancient ultratech workshop contains the lost knowledge and equipment necessary to craft cryptogear, a technology long thought to be impossible to recreate by any other means. This is our only chance to forge such items, so we must bring our best crafters and as many resources as possible.

The cryptoforge requires an immense amount of power to function, but the crash site still contains a dormant cryptogenerator. While it survived the descent, it only has enough fuel to run for 48 hours. Once activated, it will begin a countdown to meltdown, wiping the entire fragment from the planet’s surface, along with the cryptoforge. This limited time frame means we must work quickly, crafting as much cryptogear as possible before the generator’s collapse. Enabling the cryptogenerator will also reactivate the ship’s ancient defensive turrets, providing much-needed protection against the mechanoids that will inevitably arrive, drawn to the massive energy surge.

Beyond the cryptoforge, the ship’s interior appears remarkably intact. The sealed structure has preserved valuable resources, food, and medicine, as well as functional sleeping quarters, a recreation area, and other remnants of cryptoforge technology. The environment will still be harsh, but the cryptogenerator will power the ship’s heaters and lights, making the space more suitable for extended work. One cryptosleep casket remains occupied, whoever is inside has been frozen for centuries. Freeing them could prove useful, though it is not essential to our mission.

This is our one and only opportunity. Once the generator melts down, the cryptoforge and its secrets will be lost forever. We must establish a strong defense, make the most of our limited time, and craft as much legendary cryptogear as possible before the clock runs out.
LanguageData.VQE_Deadlife_AncientICBMLaunchSite.quest:
questDescription->We have intercepted a broadcast at a recently raided underground silo: Operation: Deadlife is now underway. Enough resource silos have been breached, and the plan has been set in motion. The broadcast confirms that [General_nameFull] has initiated final preparations at an ancient ICBM launch site.

Refueling of the devastating missiles is in progress. These warheads are armed with deadlife dust payloads and if launched, they will detonate high in orbit, spreading a near-permanent death pall across the planet. It is likely that endless hordes of shamblers will then scour the world, consuming or destroying all remaining civilization.

We have traced the origin of the signal to the ICBM launch facility. We must travel there and stop [General_nameFull] before the launch sequence can be completed. The site is expected to be heavily defended, not only swarming with shamblers, but also protected by restored automated defenses.

[General_nameFull] will activate numerous defensive systems from the facility’s control room: opening sealed death pits, releasing shambler animals from containment kennels, flooding the complex with rotstink gas, and awakening elite deadlife officers from cryogenic caskets.

Only by reaching and destroying the launch terminal can we prevent the missiles from firing. Failure to stop the launch in time, or ignoring this threat entirely, will doom the planet to a global shambler apocalypse.

This is our only chance to end Operation: Deadlife once and for all.
LanguageData.VQE_Deadlife_AncientSilo.questDescriptio:
questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has informed us of the location of a sealed entrance to one of the [ancientSilos] left behind during Operation: Deadlife, a planet-spanning initiative launched by a [longDeadGovernment] in preparation for global catastrophe.

These silos were designed to remain sealed for generations, [intention]. Inside, it's believed they contain [containedReward]. The deeper chambers are rumored to hold equipment and supplies capable of restarting civilization itself.

[threat]. [importantLight]. Without the light, you’ll be navigating blind and vulnerable to attack.

[commandMap]. Studying these might reveal the locations of other silos, and more importantly, bring us closer to discovering the Command Headquarters, the nerve center of Operation: Deadlife, where the truth behind this apocalyptic plan may still lie buried.

[questObjective].

questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->The ancient battle maps have revealed to us the location of a sealed entrance to one of the [ancientSilos] left behind during Operation: Deadlife, a planet-spanning initiative launched by a [longDeadGovernment] in preparation for global catastrophe.

These silos were designed to remain sealed for generations, [intention]. Inside, it's believed they contain [containedReward]. The deeper chambers are rumored to hold equipment and supplies capable of restarting civilization itself.

[threat]. [importantLight]. Without the light, you’ll be navigating blind and vulnerable to attack.

[commandMap]. Studying these might reveal the locations of other silos, and more importantly, bring us closer to discovering the Command Headquarters, the nerve center of Operation: Deadlife, where the truth behind this apocalyptic plan may still lie buried.

[questObjective].
